search_text: MV-FCOS3D++: Multi-View Camera-Only 4D Object Detection with Pretrained Monocular Backbones MV-FCOS3D++：使用预训练的单目骨干进行仅多视图相机的 4D 物体检测 In this technical report, we present our solution, dubbed MV-FCOS3D++, for the Camera-Only 3D Detection track in Waymo Open Dataset Challenge 2022. For multi-view camera-only 3D detection, methods based on bird-eye-view or 3D geometric representations can leverage the stereo cues from overlapped regions between adjacent views and directly perform 3D detection without hand-crafted post-processing. However, it lacks direct semantic supervision for 2D backbones, which can be complemented by pretraining simple monocular-based detectors. Our solution is a multi-view framework for 4D detection following this paradigm. It is built upon a simple monocular detector FCOS3D++, pretrained only with object annotations of Waymo, and converts multi-view features to a 3D grid space to detect 3D objects thereon. A dual-path neck for single-frame understanding and temporal stereo matching is devised to incorporate multi-frame information. Our method finally achieves 49.75% mAPL with a single model and wins 2nd place in the WOD challenge, without any LiDAR-based depth supervision during training. The code will be released at https://github.com/Tai-Wang/Depth-from-Motion. 在本技术报告中，我们介绍了我们的解决方案，称为 MV-FCOS3D++，用于 Waymo 开放数据集挑战赛 2022 中的仅摄像头 3D 检测赛道。对于多视图仅摄像头 3D 检测，基于鸟瞰图或 3D 几何表示的方法可以利用相邻视图之间重叠区域的立体线索，直接执行 3D 检测，无需手工制作后处理。然而，它缺乏对 2D 主干的直接语义监督，这可以通过预训练简单的基于单目的检测器来补充。我们的解决方案是遵循此范例的 4D 检测多视图框架。它基于简单的单目检测器 FCOS3D++ 构建，仅使用 Waymo 的对象注释进行预训练，并将多视图特征转换为 3D 网格空间以检测其上的 3D 对象。设计了用于单帧理解和时间立体匹配的双路径颈部来合并多帧信息。我们的方法最终使用单一模型实现了 49.75% mAPL，并在 WOD 挑战中获得第二名，并且在训练期间没有任何基于 LiDAR 的深度监督。代码将在 https://github.com/Tai-Wang/Depth-from-Motion 发布。 本文提出了一种名为MV-FCOS3D++的多视角相机仅4D目标检测方法，用于Waymo Open Dataset Challenge 2022的Camera-Only 3D Detection任务。 Tai Wang,Qing Lian,Chenming Zhu,Xinge Zhu,Wenwei Zhang cs.CV,cs.RO

authors_text: Soham Bhosale
title: Comparison of Deep Learning and Machine Learning Models and Frameworks for Skin Lesion Classification
title_zh: 皮肤病变分类的深度学习和机器学习模型和框架的比较
abstract: The incidence rate for skin cancer has been steadily increasing throughout the world, leading to it being a serious issue. Diagnosis at an early stage has the potential to drastically reduce the harm caused by the disease, however, the traditional biopsy is a labor-intensive and invasive procedure. In addition, numerous rural communities do not have easy access to hospitals and do not prefer visiting one for what they feel might be a minor issue. Using machine learning and deep learning for skin cancer classification can increase accessibility and reduce the discomforting procedures involved in the traditional lesion detection process. These models can be wrapped in web or mobile apps and serve a greater population. In this paper, two such models are tested on the benchmark HAM10000 dataset of common skin lesions. They are Random Forest with Stratified K-Fold Validation, and MobileNetV2 (throughout the rest of the paper referred to as MobileNet). The MobileNet model was trained separately using both TensorFlow and PyTorch frameworks. A side-by-side comparison of both deep learning and machine learning models and a comparison of the same deep learning model on different frameworks for skin lesion diagnosis in a resource-constrained mobile environment has not been conducted before. The results indicate that each of these models fares better at different classification tasks. For greater overall recall, accuracy, and detection of malignant melanoma, the TensorFlow MobileNet was the better choice. However, for detecting noncancerous skin lesions, the PyTorch MobileNet proved to be better. Random Forest was the better algorithm when it came to having a low computational cost with moderate correctness.

They are Random Forest with Stratified K-Fold Validation, and MobileNetV2 (throughout the rest of the paper referred to as MobileNet). The MobileNet model was trained separately using both TensorFlow and PyTorch frameworks. A side-by-side comparison of both deep learning and machine learning models and a comparison of the same deep learning model on different frameworks for skin lesion diagnosis in a resource-constrained mobile environment has not been conducted before. The results indicate that each of these models fares better at different classification tasks. For greater overall recall, accuracy, and detection of malignant melanoma, the TensorFlow MobileNet was the better choice. However, for detecting noncancerous skin lesions, the PyTorch MobileNet proved to be better. Random Forest was the better algorithm when it came to having a low computational cost with moderate correctness.
abstract_zh: The incidence rate for skin cancer has been steadily increasing throughout the world, leading to it being a serious issue.早期诊断有可能大大减少疾病造成的危害，然而，传统的活检是一种劳动密集型和侵入性程序。此外，许多农村社区去医院并不方便，也不愿意去医院，因为他们认为这可能是一个小问题。使用机器学习和深度学习进行皮肤癌分类可以提高可访问性并减少传统病变检测过程中涉及的令人不适的程序。 These models can be wrapped in web or mobile apps and serve a greater population. In this paper, two such models are tested on the benchmark HAM10000 dataset of common skin lesions. They are Random Forest with Stratified K-Fold Validation, and MobileNetV2 (throughout the rest of the paper referred to as MobileNet). MobileNet 模型是使用 TensorFlow 和 PyTorch 框架单独训练的。之前还没有对深度学习和机器学习模型进行并列比较，以及在资源有限的移动环境中相同的深度学习模型在不同框架上进行皮肤病变诊断的比较。 The results indicate that each of these models fares better at different classification tasks.为了提高恶性黑色素瘤的整体召回率、准确性和检测率，TensorFlow MobileNet 是更好的选择。 However, for detecting noncancerous skin lesions, the PyTorch MobileNet proved to be better. Random Forest was the better algorithm when it came to having a low computational cost with moderate correctness.

AI.tldr: 本文对比了深度学习和机器学习模型在皮肤病变分类中的性能，使用HAM10000数据集测试了随机森林、TensorFlow和PyTorch框架下的MobileNetV2模型。
AI.motivation: 皮肤癌发病率上升，传统活检方法存在侵入性和可及性问题，需通过机器学习和深度学习提高诊断的可及性并减少侵入性流程。
AI.method: 在HAM10000数据集上测试随机森林（带分层K折验证）和MobileNetV2模型（分别用TensorFlow和PyTorch实现），比较不同模型及框架在皮肤病变分类中的表现。
AI.result: TensorFlow MobileNet在总体召回率、准确率和恶性黑色素瘤检测中表现更优；PyTorch MobileNet在非癌性病变检测中更优；随机森林计算成本低但正确率适中。
AI.conclusion: 不同模型适用于不同分类任务，TensorFlow适合恶性病变检测，PyTorch适合非癌性病变，随机森林适合资源受限场景。

authors_text: Michael Polyntsov,Valentin Grigorev,Kirill Smirnov,George Chernishev
title: Implementing the Comparison-Based External Sort
title_zh: 实现基于比较的外部排序
abstract: In the age of big data, sorting is an indispensable operation for DBMSes and similar systems. Having data sorted can help produce query plans with significantly lower run times. It also can provide other benefits like having non-blocking operators which will produce data steadily (without bursts), or operators with reduced memory footprint. Sorting may be required on any step of query processing, i.e., be it source data or intermediate results. At the same time, the data to be sorted may not fit into main memory. In this case, an external sort operator, which writes intermediate results to disk, should be used. In this paper we consider an external sort operator of the comparison-based sort type. We discuss its implementation and describe related design decisions. Our aim is to study the impact on performance of a data structure used on the merge step. For this, we have experimentally evaluated three data structures implemented inside a DBMS. Results have shown that it is worthwhile to make an effort to implement an efficient data structure for run merging, even on modern commodity computers which are usually disk-bound. Moreover, we demonstrated that using a loser tree is a more efficient approach than both the naive approach and the heap-based one.
summary: In the age of big data, sorting is an indispensable operation for DBMSes and similar systems. Having data sorted can help produce query plans with significantly lower run times. It also can provide other benefits like having non-blocking operators which will produce data steadily (without bursts), or operators with reduced memory footprint. Sorting may be required on any step of query processing, i.e., be it source data or intermediate results. At the same time, the data to be sorted may not fit into main memory. In this case, an external sort operator, which writes intermediate results to disk, should be used. In this paper we consider an external sort operator of the comparison-based sort type. We discuss its implementation and describe related design decisions. Our aim is to study the impact on performance of a data structure used on the merge step. For this, we have experimentally evaluated three data structures implemented inside a DBMS. Results have shown that it is worthwhile to make an effort to implement an efficient data structure for run merging, even on modern commodity computers which are usually disk-bound. Moreover, we demonstrated that using a loser tree is a more efficient approach than both the naive approach and the heap-based one.
abstract_zh: 在大数据时代，排序是 DBMS 和类似系统不可或缺的操作。对数据进行排序有助于生成运行时间显着缩短的查询计划。它还可以提供其他好处，例如拥有可稳定生成数据（无突发）的非阻塞运算符，或减少内存占用的运算符。查询处理的任何步骤都可能需要排序，即无论是源数据还是中间结果。同时，要排序的数据可能无法装入主存储器。在这种情况下，应使用外部排序运算符，将中间结果写入磁盘。在本文中，我们考虑基于比较的排序类型的外部排序运算符。我们讨论其实现并描述相关的设计决策。我们的目的是研究合并步骤中使用的数据结构对性能的影响。为此，我们通过实验评估了 DBMS 内实现的三种数据结构。结果表明，即使在通常受磁盘限制的现代商品计算机上，努力实现用于运行合并的有效数据结构也是值得的。此外，我们证明了使用失败者树是一种比朴素方法和基于堆的方法更有效的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文研究比较型外部排序的实现，重点分析合并阶段数据结构对性能的影响，并通过实验验证败者树的效率优势。
AI.motivation: 大数据场景下排序操作至关重要，但数据可能无法完全加载到内存，需依赖外部排序。现有方法的合并效率存在优化空间，需探索更优数据结构。
AI.method: 在数据库系统中实现三种数据结构（败者树、朴素方法、堆方法），通过实验对比其在合并阶段的性能表现。
AI.result: 实验表明，败者树在现代计算机上比朴素方法和堆方法更高效，验证了优化数据结构对提升外部排序性能的价值。
AI.conclusion: 即使在磁盘受限的现代硬件环境下，针对合并阶段优化数据结构（如采用败者树）仍能显著提升外部排序效率，值得投入开发。

tldr: 本文研究比较型外部排序的实现，重点分析合并阶段数据结构对性能的影响，并通过实验验证败者树的效率优势。
motivation: 大数据场景下排序操作至关重要，但数据可能无法完全加载到内存，需依赖外部排序。现有方法的合并效率存在优化空间，需探索更优数据结构。
method: 在数据库系统中实现三种数据结构（败者树、朴素方法、堆方法），通过实验对比其在合并阶段的性能表现。
result: 实验表明，败者树在现代计算机上比朴素方法和堆方法更高效，验证了优化数据结构对提升外部排序性能的价值。
conclusion: 即使在磁盘受限的现代硬件环境下，针对合并阶段优化数据结构（如采用败者树）仍能显著提升外部排序效率，值得投入开发。
search_text: Implementing the Comparison-Based External Sort 实现基于比较的外部排序 In the age of big data, sorting is an indispensable operation for DBMSes and similar systems. Having data sorted can help produce query plans with significantly lower run times. It also can provide other benefits like having non-blocking operators which will produce data steadily (without bursts), or operators with reduced memory footprint. Sorting may be required on any step of query processing, i.e., be it source data or intermediate results. At the same time, the data to be sorted may not fit into main memory. In this case, an external sort operator, which writes intermediate results to disk, should be used. In this paper we consider an external sort operator of the comparison-based sort type. We discuss its implementation and describe related design decisions. Our aim is to study the impact on performance of a data structure used on the merge step. For this, we have experimentally evaluated three data structures implemented inside a DBMS. Results have shown that it is worthwhile to make an effort to implement an efficient data structure for run merging, even on modern commodity computers which are usually disk-bound. Moreover, we demonstrated that using a loser tree is a more efficient approach than both the naive approach and the heap-based one. 在大数据时代，排序是 DBMS 和类似系统不可或缺的操作。对数据进行排序有助于生成运行时间显着缩短的查询计划。它还可以提供其他好处，例如拥有可稳定生成数据（无突发）的非阻塞运算符，或减少内存占用的运算符。查询处理的任何步骤都可能需要排序，即无论是源数据还是中间结果。同时，要排序的数据可能无法装入主存储器。在这种情况下，应使用外部排序运算符，将中间结果写入磁盘。在本文中，我们考虑基于比较的排序类型的外部排序运算符。我们讨论其实现并描述相关的设计决策。我们的目的是研究合并步骤中使用的数据结构对性能的影响。为此，我们通过实验评估了 DBMS 内实现的三种数据结构。结果表明，即使在通常受磁盘限制的现代商品计算机上，努力实现用于运行合并的有效数据结构也是值得的。此外，我们证明了使用失败者树是一种比朴素方法和基于堆的方法更有效的方法。 本文研究比较型外部排序的实现，重点分析合并阶段数据结构对性能的影响，并通过实验验证败者树的效率优势。 Michael Polyntsov,Valentin Grigorev,Kirill Smirnov,George Chernishev cs.DB,cs.DS,cs.PF

authors_text: Ye Wang,Jingbo Liao,Hong Yu,Guoyin Wang,Xiaoxia Zhang,Li Liu
title: Advanced Conditional Variational Autoencoders (A-CVAE): Towards interpreting open-domain conversation generation via disentangling latent feature representation
title_zh: 高级条件变分自动编码器（A-CVAE）：通过解开潜在特征表示来解释开放域对话生成
abstract: Currently end-to-end deep learning based open-domain dialogue systems remain black box models, making it easy to generate irrelevant contents with data-driven models. Specifically, latent variables are highly entangled with different semantics in the latent space due to the lack of priori knowledge to guide the training. To address this problem, this paper proposes to harness the generative model with a priori knowledge through a cognitive approach involving mesoscopic scale feature disentanglement. Particularly, the model integrates the macro-level guided-category knowledge and micro-level open-domain dialogue data for the training, leveraging the priori knowledge into the latent space, which enables the model to disentangle the latent variables within the mesoscopic scale. Besides, we propose a new metric for open-domain dialogues, which can objectively evaluate the interpretability of the latent space distribution. Finally, we validate our model on different datasets and experimentally demonstrate that our model is able to generate higher quality and more interpretable dialogues than other models.
summary: Currently end-to-end deep learning based open-domain dialogue systems remain black box models, making it easy to generate irrelevant contents with data-driven models. Specifically, latent variables are highly entangled with different semantics in the latent space due to the lack of priori knowledge to guide the training. To address this problem, this paper proposes to harness the generative model with a priori knowledge through a cognitive approach involving mesoscopic scale feature disentanglement. Particularly, the model integrates the macro-level guided-category knowledge and micro-level open-domain dialogue data for the training, leveraging the priori knowledge into the latent space, which enables the model to disentangle the latent variables within the mesoscopic scale. Besides, we propose a new metric for open-domain dialogues, which can objectively evaluate the interpretability of the latent space distribution. Finally, we validate our model on different datasets and experimentally demonstrate that our model is able to generate higher quality and more interpretable dialogues than other models.
abstract_zh: 目前基于端到端深度学习的开放域对话系统仍然是黑盒模型，很容易用数据驱动的模型生成不相关的内容。具体来说，由于缺乏指导训练的先验知识，潜在变量与潜在空间中的不同语义高度纠缠在一起。为了解决这个问题，本文提出通过涉及介观尺度特征解缠的认知方法来利用具有先验知识的生成模型。特别是，该模型集成了宏观层面的引导类别知识和微观层面的开放域对话数据进行训练，将先验知识引入潜在空间，使模型能够在介观尺度内解开潜在变量。此外，我们提出了一种新的开放域对话度量，可以客观地评估潜在空间分布的可解释性。最后，我们在不同的数据集上验证我们的模型，并通过实验证明我们的模型能够比其他模型生成更高质量和更可解释的对话。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种改进的条件变分自编码器（A-CVAE），通过解耦潜在特征表示来提升开放域对话生成的可解释性。
AI.motivation: 现有端到端开放域对话系统为黑箱模型，生成内容易偏离主题且潜在变量语义高度纠缠，缺乏先验知识指导导致可解释性差。
AI.method: 融合宏观指导类别知识与微观对话数据，将先验知识注入潜在空间实现中观尺度解耦，并提出新的可解释性评估指标。
AI.result: 实验表明该模型在多个数据集上生成的对话质量更高，潜在空间分布更易解释，优于现有模型。
AI.conclusion: A-CVAE通过解耦潜在特征有效提升了开放域对话生成的可解释性与质量，为对话系统研究提供了新思路。

authors_text: Hui-Xian Cheng,Xian-Feng Han,Guo-Qiang Xiao
title: CENet: Toward Concise and Efficient LiDAR Semantic Segmentation for Autonomous Driving
title_zh: CENet：面向自动驾驶的简洁高效的 LiDAR 语义分割
abstract: Accurate and fast scene understanding is one of the challenging task for autonomous driving, which requires to take full advantage of LiDAR point clouds for semantic segmentation. In this paper, we present a \textbf{concise} and \textbf{efficient} image-based semantic segmentation network, named \textbf{CENet}. In order to improve the descriptive power of learned features and reduce the computational as well as time complexity, our CENet integrates the convolution with larger kernel size instead of MLP, carefully-selected activation functions, and multiple auxiliary segmentation heads with corresponding loss functions into architecture. Quantitative and qualitative experiments conducted on publicly available benchmarks, SemanticKITTI and SemanticPOSS, demonstrate that our pipeline achieves much better mIoU and inference performance compared with state-of-the-art models. The code will be available at https://github.com/huixiancheng/CENet.
summary: Accurate and fast scene understanding is one of the challenging task for autonomous driving, which requires to take full advantage of LiDAR point clouds for semantic segmentation. In this paper, we present a \textbf{concise} and \textbf{efficient} image-based semantic segmentation network, named \textbf{CENet}. In order to improve the descriptive power of learned features and reduce the computational as well as time complexity, our CENet integrates the convolution with larger kernel size instead of MLP, carefully-selected activation functions, and multiple auxiliary segmentation heads with corresponding loss functions into architecture. Quantitative and qualitative experiments conducted on publicly available benchmarks, SemanticKITTI and SemanticPOSS, demonstrate that our pipeline achieves much better mIoU and inference performance compared with state-of-the-art models. The code will be available at https://github.com/huixiancheng/CENet.
abstract_zh: 准确快速的场景理解是自动驾驶的挑战性任务之一，需要充分利用激光雷达点云进行语义分割。在本文中，我们提出了一个\textbf{简洁}和\textbf{高效}基于图像的语义分割网络，命名为\textbf{CENet}。为了提高学习特征的描述能力并降低计算和时间复杂度，我们的 CENet 将具有更大内核尺寸的卷积（而不是 MLP）、精心选择的激活函数以及具有相应损失函数的多个辅助分割头集成到架构中。在公开的基准、SemanticKITTI 和 SemanticPOSS 上进行的定量和定性实验表明，与最先进的模型相比，我们的流程实现了更好的 mIoU 和推理性能。代码可在 https://github.com/huixian Cheng/CENet 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为CENet的高效且简洁的基于图像的LiDAR语义分割网络，用于自动驾驶场景理解。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶需要快速准确的场景理解，传统方法在计算复杂度和特征描述能力上存在不足，需优化LiDAR点云语义分割效率。
AI.method: 采用大核卷积替代MLP、精心选择激活函数，并引入多辅助分割头与对应损失函数，以提升特征表达能力并降低计算复杂度。
AI.result: 在SemanticKITTI和SemanticPOSS基准测试中，CENet在mIoU和推理速度上均优于现有最先进模型。
AI.conclusion: CENet通过架构创新实现了高效且高精度的LiDAR语义分割，为自动驾驶提供了更优的解决方案。

authors_text: Efthyvoulos Drousiotis,Paul G. Spirakis
title: Single MCMC Chain Parallelisation on Decision Trees
title_zh: 决策树上的单 MCMC 链并行化
abstract: Decision trees are highly famous in machine learning and usually acquire state-of-the-art performance. Despite that, well-known variants like CART, ID3, random forest, and boosted trees miss a probabilistic version that encodes prior assumptions about tree structures and shares statistical strength between node parameters. Existing work on Bayesian decision trees depend on Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC), which can be computationally slow, especially on high dimensional data and expensive proposals. In this study, we propose a method to parallelise a single MCMC decision tree chain on an average laptop or personal computer that enables us to reduce its run-time through multi-core processing while the results are statistically identical to conventional sequential implementation. We also calculate the theoretical and practical reduction in run time, which can be obtained utilising our method on multi-processor architectures. Experiments showed that we could achieve 18 times faster running time provided that the serial and the parallel implementation are statistically identical.
summary: Decision trees are highly famous in machine learning and usually acquire state-of-the-art performance. Despite that, well-known variants like CART, ID3, random forest, and boosted trees miss a probabilistic version that encodes prior assumptions about tree structures and shares statistical strength between node parameters. Existing work on Bayesian decision trees depend on Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC), which can be computationally slow, especially on high dimensional data and expensive proposals. In this study, we propose a method to parallelise a single MCMC decision tree chain on an average laptop or personal computer that enables us to reduce its run-time through multi-core processing while the results are statistically identical to conventional sequential implementation. We also calculate the theoretical and practical reduction in run time, which can be obtained utilising our method on multi-processor architectures. Experiments showed that we could achieve 18 times faster running time provided that the serial and the parallel implementation are statistically identical.
abstract_zh: 决策树在机器学习中非常有名，并且通常能够获得最先进的性能。尽管如此，CART、ID3、随机森林和提升树等众所周知的变体都缺少一个概率版本，该版本对有关树结构的先前假设进行编码并在节点参数之间共享统计强度。贝叶斯决策树的现有工作依赖于马尔可夫链蒙特卡罗（MCMC），它的计算速度可能很慢，特别是在高维数据和昂贵的建议上。在本研究中，我们提出了一种在普通笔记本电脑或个人计算机上并行化单个 MCMC 决策树链的方法，使我们能够通过多核处理减少其运行时间，同时结果在统计上与传统的顺序实现相同。我们还计算了运行时间的理论和实际减少量，这可以利用我们在多处理器架构上的方法来获得。实验表明，如果串行和并行实现在统计上相同，我们可以将运行时间提高 18 倍。

AI.tldr: 提出一种在普通设备上通过多核处理并行化单个MCMC决策树链的方法，以减少运行时间而不损失统计一致性。
AI.motivation: 现有贝叶斯决策树依赖MCMC计算速度慢，尤其在高维数据和昂贵提议步骤时，需更高效方法。
AI.method: 通过多核处理并行化单个MCMC链，利用多处理器架构减少运行时间，同时确保结果与串行实现统计一致。
AI.result: 实验显示在保持统计一致性下，运行时间加快18倍。
AI.conclusion: 证明在普通设备上并行化单个MCMC链的可行性，为贝叶斯决策树提供高效解决方案。

search_text: Single MCMC Chain Parallelisation on Decision Trees 决策树上的单 MCMC 链并行化 Decision trees are highly famous in machine learning and usually acquire state-of-the-art performance. Despite that, well-known variants like CART, ID3, random forest, and boosted trees miss a probabilistic version that encodes prior assumptions about tree structures and shares statistical strength between node parameters. Existing work on Bayesian decision trees depend on Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC), which can be computationally slow, especially on high dimensional data and expensive proposals. In this study, we propose a method to parallelise a single MCMC decision tree chain on an average laptop or personal computer that enables us to reduce its run-time through multi-core processing while the results are statistically identical to conventional sequential implementation. We also calculate the theoretical and practical reduction in run time, which can be obtained utilising our method on multi-processor architectures. Experiments showed that we could achieve 18 times faster running time provided that the serial and the parallel implementation are statistically identical. 决策树在机器学习中非常有名，并且通常能够获得最先进的性能。尽管如此，CART、ID3、随机森林和提升树等众所周知的变体都缺少一个概率版本，该版本对有关树结构的先前假设进行编码并在节点参数之间共享统计强度。贝叶斯决策树的现有工作依赖于马尔可夫链蒙特卡罗（MCMC），它的计算速度可能很慢，特别是在高维数据和昂贵的建议上。在本研究中，我们提出了一种在普通笔记本电脑或个人计算机上并行化单个 MCMC 决策树链的方法，使我们能够通过多核处理减少其运行时间，同时结果在统计上与传统的顺序实现相同。我们还计算了运行时间的理论和实际减少量，这可以利用我们在多处理器架构上的方法来获得。实验表明，如果串行和并行实现在统计上相同，我们可以将运行时间提高 18 倍。 提出一种在普通设备上通过多核处理并行化单个MCMC决策树链的方法，以减少运行时间而不损失统计一致性。 Efthyvoulos Drousiotis,Paul G. Spirakis cs.AI,cs.DC

authors_text: Martha Paskin,Daniel Baum,Mason N. Dean,Christoph von Tycowicz
title: A Kendall Shape Space Approach to 3D Shape Estimation from 2D Landmarks
title_zh: 从 2D 地标进行 3D 形状估计的 Kendall 形状空间方法
comment: to appear in European Conference on Computer Vision 2022
comments: to appear in European Conference on Computer Vision 2022
abstract: 3D shapes provide substantially more information than 2D images. However, the acquisition of 3D shapes is sometimes very difficult or even impossible in comparison with acquiring 2D images, making it necessary to derive the 3D shape from 2D images. Although this is, in general, a mathematically ill-posed problem, it might be solved by constraining the problem formulation using prior information. Here, we present a new approach based on Kendall's shape space to reconstruct 3D shapes from single monocular 2D images. The work is motivated by an application to study the feeding behavior of the basking shark, an endangered species whose massive size and mobility render 3D shape data nearly impossible to obtain, hampering understanding of their feeding behaviors and ecology. 2D images of these animals in feeding position, however, are readily available. We compare our approach with state-of-the-art shape-based approaches, both on human stick models and on shark head skeletons. Using a small set of training shapes, we show that the Kendall shape space approach is substantially more robust than previous methods and results in plausible shapes. This is essential for the motivating application in which specimens are rare and therefore only few training shapes are available.
summary: 3D shapes provide substantially more information than 2D images. However, the acquisition of 3D shapes is sometimes very difficult or even impossible in comparison with acquiring 2D images, making it necessary to derive the 3D shape from 2D images. Although this is, in general, a mathematically ill-posed problem, it might be solved by constraining the problem formulation using prior information. Here, we present a new approach based on Kendall's shape space to reconstruct 3D shapes from single monocular 2D images. The work is motivated by an application to study the feeding behavior of the basking shark, an endangered species whose massive size and mobility render 3D shape data nearly impossible to obtain, hampering understanding of their feeding behaviors and ecology. 2D images of these animals in feeding position, however, are readily available. We compare our approach with state-of-the-art shape-based approaches, both on human stick models and on shark head skeletons. Using a small set of training shapes, we show that the Kendall shape space approach is substantially more robust than previous methods and results in plausible shapes. This is essential for the motivating application in which specimens are rare and therefore only few training shapes are available.
abstract_zh: 3D 形状比 2D 图像提供更多的信息。然而，与获取 2D 图像相比，获取 3D 形状有时非常困难甚至不可能，因此有必要从 2D 图像导出 3D 形状。虽然这通常是一个数学不适定问题，但它可以通过使用先验信息约束问题表述来解决。在这里，我们提出了一种基于 Kendall 形状空间的新方法，用于从单个单眼 2D 图像重建 3D 形状。这项工作的动机是研究姥鲨的摄食行为，姥鲨是一种濒临灭绝的物种，其巨大的体型和移动性使得 3D 形状数据几乎不可能获得，从而妨碍了对其摄食行为和生态的了解。然而，这些动物在进食位置的二维图像很容易获得。我们将我们的方法与最先进的基于形状的方法进行比较，无论是在人类棍棒模型还是鲨鱼头骨骼上。使用一小组训练形状，我们表明肯德尔形状空间方法比以前的方法更加稳健，并且可以产生合理的形状。这对于激励应用至关重要，因为样本很少，因此只有很少的训练形状可用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Kendall形状空间的方法，从单目2D图像中的地标点重建3D形状，特别针对难以获取3D数据的濒危物种（如座头鲨）的进食行为研究。
AI.motivation: 座头鲨等濒危物种因体型庞大和活动性强，难以直接获取3D形状数据，而2D图像易得但信息有限，需通过算法从2D图像推断3D形状以研究其生态行为。
AI.method: 利用Kendall形状空间理论，结合先验形状信息约束逆问题求解，通过少量训练样本（如人类骨架模型和鲨鱼头骨）验证方法有效性。
AI.result: 该方法在训练样本极少的情况下表现出比现有方法更高的鲁棒性，生成的3D形状合理，适用于稀有样本的形态分析场景。
AI.conclusion: Kendall形状空间方法为从2D图像重建3D形状提供了有效解决方案，尤其适合数据稀缺的生物形态学研究领域。

authors_text: Xinbin Liang,Yang Yu,Yadong Liu,Kaixuan Liu,Yaru Liu,Zongtan Zhou
title: EEG-Based Detection of Braking Intention During Simulated Driving
title_zh: 基于脑电图的模拟驾驶制动意图检测
abstract: Accurately detecting and identifying drivers' braking intention is the basis of man-machine driving. In this paper, we proposed an electroencephalographic (EEG)-based braking intention measurement strategy. We used the Car Learning to Act (Carla) platform to build the simulated driving environment. 11 subjects participated in our study, and each subject drove a simulated vehicle to complete emergency braking and normal braking tasks. We compared the EEG topographic maps in different braking situations and used three different classifiers to predict the subjects' braking intention through EEG signals. The experimental results showed that the average response time of subjects in emergency braking was 762 ms; emergency braking and no braking can be well distinguished, while normal braking and no braking were not easy to be classified; for the two different types of braking, emergency braking and normal braking had obvious differences in EEG topographic maps, and the classification results also showed that the two were highly distinguishable. This study provides a user-centered driver-assistance system and a good framework to combine with advanced shared control algorithms, which has the potential to be applied to achieve a more friendly interaction between the driver and vehicle in real driving environment.
summary: Accurately detecting and identifying drivers' braking intention is the basis of man-machine driving. In this paper, we proposed an electroencephalographic (EEG)-based braking intention measurement strategy. We used the Car Learning to Act (Carla) platform to build the simulated driving environment. 11 subjects participated in our study, and each subject drove a simulated vehicle to complete emergency braking and normal braking tasks. We compared the EEG topographic maps in different braking situations and used three different classifiers to predict the subjects' braking intention through EEG signals. The experimental results showed that the average response time of subjects in emergency braking was 762 ms; emergency braking and no braking can be well distinguished, while normal braking and no braking were not easy to be classified; for the two different types of braking, emergency braking and normal braking had obvious differences in EEG topographic maps, and the classification results also showed that the two were highly distinguishable. This study provides a user-centered driver-assistance system and a good framework to combine with advanced shared control algorithms, which has the potential to be applied to achieve a more friendly interaction between the driver and vehicle in real driving environment.
abstract_zh: 准确检测和识别驾驶员的制动意图是人机驾驶的基础。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于脑电图（EEG）的制动意图测量策略。我们使用 Car Learning to Act (Carla) 平台来构建模拟驾驶环境。 11名受试者参与了我们的研究，每个受试者驾驶模拟车辆完成紧急制动和正常制动任务。我们比较了不同制动情况下的脑电图地形图，并使用三种不同的分类器通过脑电图信号预测受试者的制动意图。实验结果显示，受试者在紧急制动时的平均反应时间为762毫秒；紧急制动和不制动可以很好区分，而正常制动和不制动则不易区分；对于两种不同类型的制动，紧急制动和正常制动在脑电地形图上存在明显差异，分类结果也表明两者具有较高的可区分性。本研究提供了一个以用户为中心的驾驶辅助系统，并提供了一个与先进共享控制算法相结合的良好框架，有潜力应用于在真实驾驶环境中实现驾驶员与车辆之间更友好的交互。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于脑电图（EEG）的紧急制动意图检测方法，并在模拟驾驶环境中验证其有效性。
AI.motivation: 准确检测驾驶员制动意图是实现人车协同驾驶的基础，传统方法存在局限性，需探索更精准的生物信号检测技术。
AI.method: 使用CARLA平台构建模拟驾驶环境，收集11名受试者的EEG数据，通过对比不同制动场景的脑电地形图，并采用三种分类器预测制动意图。
AI.result: 实验显示紧急制动平均反应时间为762ms，紧急制动与无制动可有效区分，正常制动与无制动区分困难；两种制动类型在EEG地形图上差异显著且分类准确率高。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为用户中心的驾驶辅助系统提供了框架，可结合共享控制算法实现更友好的人车交互，具有实际应用潜力。

ai_content.result: 实验显示紧急制动平均反应时间为762ms，紧急制动与无制动可有效区分，正常制动与无制动区分困难；两种制动类型在EEG地形图上差异显著且分类准确率高。

ai_content_json.method: 使用CARLA平台构建模拟驾驶环境，收集11名受试者的EEG数据，通过对比不同制动场景的脑电地形图，并采用三种分类器预测制动意图。
ai_content_json.result: 实验显示紧急制动平均反应时间为762ms，紧急制动与无制动可有效区分，正常制动与无制动区分困难；两种制动类型在EEG地形图上差异显著且分类准确率高。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 该研究为用户中心的驾驶辅助系统提供了框架，可结合共享控制算法实现更友好的人车交互，具有实际应用潜力。

search_text: EEG-Based Detection of Braking Intention During Simulated Driving 基于脑电图的模拟驾驶制动意图检测 Accurately detecting and identifying drivers' braking intention is the basis of man-machine driving. In this paper, we proposed an electroencephalographic (EEG)-based braking intention measurement strategy. We used the Car Learning to Act (Carla) platform to build the simulated driving environment. 11 subjects participated in our study, and each subject drove a simulated vehicle to complete emergency braking and normal braking tasks. We compared the EEG topographic maps in different braking situations and used three different classifiers to predict the subjects' braking intention through EEG signals. The experimental results showed that the average response time of subjects in emergency braking was 762 ms; emergency braking and no braking can be well distinguished, while normal braking and no braking were not easy to be classified; for the two different types of braking, emergency braking and normal braking had obvious differences in EEG topographic maps, and the classification results also showed that the two were highly distinguishable. This study provides a user-centered driver-assistance system and a good framework to combine with advanced shared control algorithms, which has the potential to be applied to achieve a more friendly interaction between the driver and vehicle in real driving environment. 准确检测和识别驾驶员的制动意图是人机驾驶的基础。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于脑电图（EEG）的制动意图测量策略。我们使用 Car Learning to Act (Carla) 平台来构建模拟驾驶环境。 11名受试者参与了我们的研究，每个受试者驾驶模拟车辆完成紧急制动和正常制动任务。我们比较了不同制动情况下的脑电图地形图，并使用三种不同的分类器通过脑电图信号预测受试者的制动意图。实验结果显示，受试者在紧急制动时的平均反应时间为762毫秒；紧急制动和不制动可以很好区分，而正常制动和不制动则不易区分；对于两种不同类型的制动，紧急制动和正常制动在脑电地形图上存在明显差异，分类结果也表明两者具有较高的可区分性。本研究提供了一个以用户为中心的驾驶辅助系统，并提供了一个与先进共享控制算法相结合的良好框架，有潜力应用于在真实驾驶环境中实现驾驶员与车辆之间更友好的交互。 本文提出了一种基于脑电图（EEG）的紧急制动意图检测方法，并在模拟驾驶环境中验证其有效性。 Xinbin Liang,Yang Yu,Yadong Liu,Kaixuan Liu,Yaru Liu,Zongtan Zhou cs.HC

authors_text: Guangyao Dou,Zheng Zhou,Xiaodong Qu
title: Time Majority Voting, a PC-based EEG Classifier for Non-expert Users
title_zh: 时间多数投票，适用于非专家用户的基于 PC 的脑电图分类器
abstract: Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning to predict cognitive tasks from electroencephalography (EEG) signals is a rapidly advancing field in Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI). In contrast to the fields of computer vision and natural language processing, the data amount of these trials is still rather tiny. Developing a PC-based machine learning technique to increase the participation of non-expert end-users could help solve this data collection issue. We created a novel algorithm for machine learning called Time Majority Voting (TMV). In our experiment, TMV performed better than cutting-edge algorithms. It can operate efficiently on personal computers for classification tasks involving the BCI. These interpretable data also assisted end-users and researchers in comprehending EEG tests better.
summary: Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning to predict cognitive tasks from electroencephalography (EEG) signals is a rapidly advancing field in Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI). In contrast to the fields of computer vision and natural language processing, the data amount of these trials is still rather tiny. Developing a PC-based machine learning technique to increase the participation of non-expert end-users could help solve this data collection issue. We created a novel algorithm for machine learning called Time Majority Voting (TMV). In our experiment, TMV performed better than cutting-edge algorithms. It can operate efficiently on personal computers for classification tasks involving the BCI. These interpretable data also assisted end-users and researchers in comprehending EEG tests better.
abstract_zh: 使用机器学习和深度学习根据脑电图 (EEG) 信号预测认知任务是脑机接口 (BCI) 领域快速发展的领域。 In contrast to the fields of computer vision and natural language processing, the data amount of these trials is still rather tiny.开发基于 PC 的机器学习技术来增加非专家最终用户的参与可能有助于解决这一数据收集问题。 We created a novel algorithm for machine learning called Time Majority Voting (TMV). In our experiment, TMV performed better than cutting-edge algorithms. It can operate efficiently on personal computers for classification tasks involving the BCI. These interpretable data also assisted end-users and researchers in comprehending EEG tests better.

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于PC的EEG分类算法Time Majority Voting（TMV），旨在提高非专家用户在脑机接口任务中的数据参与度。
AI.motivation: BCI领域EEG数据量不足，传统方法依赖专家操作限制了数据收集，需开发非专家可使用的高效分类技术。
AI.method: 设计新型机器学习算法TMV，通过时间维度多数投票机制实现EEG信号分类，并优化其在个人计算机上的运行效率。
AI.result: 实验表明TMV优于现有算法，可在PC端高效运行，且其可解释性帮助用户和研究者更好理解EEG测试过程。
AI.conclusion: TMV有效解决了非专家用户参与BCI数据收集的难题，同时提升了分类性能和数据可解释性。

authors_text: Haoxuan You,Luowei Zhou,Bin Xiao,Noel Codella,Yu Cheng,Ruochen Xu,Shih-Fu Chang,Lu Yuan
title: Learning Visual Representation from Modality-Shared Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training
title_zh: 从模态共享对比语言-图像预训练中学习视觉表示
abstract: Large-scale multi-modal contrastive pre-training has demonstrated great utility to learn transferable features for a range of downstream tasks by mapping multiple modalities into a shared embedding space. Typically, this has employed separate encoders for each modality. However, recent work suggests that transformers can support learning across multiple modalities and allow knowledge sharing. Inspired by this, we investigate a variety of Modality-Shared Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (MS-CLIP) frameworks. More specifically, we question how many parameters of a transformer model can be shared across modalities during contrastive pre-training, and rigorously examine architectural design choices that position the proportion of parameters shared along a spectrum. In studied conditions, we observe that a mostly unified encoder for vision and language signals outperforms all other variations that separate more parameters. Additionally, we find that light-weight modality-specific parallel modules further improve performance. Experimental results show that the proposed MS-CLIP approach outperforms vanilla CLIP by up to 13\% relative in zero-shot ImageNet classification (pre-trained on YFCC-100M), while simultaneously supporting a reduction of parameters. In addition, our approach outperforms vanilla CLIP by 1.6 points in linear probing on a collection of 24 downstream vision tasks. Furthermore, we discover that sharing parameters leads to semantic concepts from different modalities being encoded more closely in the embedding space, facilitating the transferring of common semantic structure (e.g., attention patterns) from language to vision. Code is available at \href{https://github.com/Hxyou/MSCLIP}{URL}.
summary: Large-scale multi-modal contrastive pre-training has demonstrated great utility to learn transferable features for a range of downstream tasks by mapping multiple modalities into a shared embedding space. Typically, this has employed separate encoders for each modality. However, recent work suggests that transformers can support learning across multiple modalities and allow knowledge sharing. Inspired by this, we investigate a variety of Modality-Shared Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (MS-CLIP) frameworks. More specifically, we question how many parameters of a transformer model can be shared across modalities during contrastive pre-training, and rigorously examine architectural design choices that position the proportion of parameters shared along a spectrum. In studied conditions, we observe that a mostly unified encoder for vision and language signals outperforms all other variations that separate more parameters. Additionally, we find that light-weight modality-specific parallel modules further improve performance. Experimental results show that the proposed MS-CLIP approach outperforms vanilla CLIP by up to 13\% relative in zero-shot ImageNet classification (pre-trained on YFCC-100M), while simultaneously supporting a reduction of parameters. In addition, our approach outperforms vanilla CLIP by 1.6 points in linear probing on a collection of 24 downstream vision tasks. Furthermore, we discover that sharing parameters leads to semantic concepts from different modalities being encoded more closely in the embedding space, facilitating the transferring of common semantic structure (e.g., attention patterns) from language to vision. Code is available at \href{https://github.com/Hxyou/MSCLIP}{URL}.
abstract_zh: 大规模多模态对比预训练已证明通过将多种模态映射到共享嵌入空间来学习一系列下游任务的可转移特征具有很大的实用性。通常，这对每种模态采用单独的编码器。然而，最近的研究表明，变压器可以支持多种模式的学习并允许知识共享。受此启发，我们研究了各种模态共享对比语言图像预训练（MS-CLIP）框架。更具体地说，我们询问在对比预训练期间可以跨模态共享 Transformer 模型的多少参数，并严格检查架构设计选择，以确定沿频谱共享的参数比例。在研究条件下，我们观察到视觉和语言信号的基本统一的编码器优于分离更多参数的所有其他变体。此外，我们发现轻量级特定于模态的并行模块进一步提高了性能。实验结果表明，所提出的 MS-CLIP 方法在零样本 ImageNet 分类（在 YFCC-100M 上预训练）中相对于普通 CLIP 的性能提高了 13%，同时支持参数的减少。此外，在 24 个下游视觉任务的线性探测中，我们的方法比普通 CLIP 的性能高出 1.6 个点。此外，我们发现共享参数导致来自不同模态的语义概念在嵌入空间中被更紧密地编码，从而促进公共语义结构（例如，注意力模式）从语言到视觉的转移。代码可在 \href{https://github.com/Hxyou/MSCLIP}{URL} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种跨模态共享参数的对比语言-图像预训练框架MS-CLIP，通过统一编码器和轻量级模块提升视觉表征学习效果。
AI.motivation: 传统多模态预训练使用独立编码器效率低，而Transformer的跨模态知识共享潜力未被充分探索，需研究参数共享比例对性能的影响。
AI.method: 设计共享参数比例可调的MS-CLIP架构，采用统一视觉-语言编码器并引入轻量级模态特定模块，在YFCC-100M数据集上进行对比预训练。
AI.result: MS-CLIP在零样本ImageNet分类任务上较CLIP提升13%，24个下游视觉任务线性探测提升1.6点，同时减少参数量，且语义嵌入空间更紧密。
AI.conclusion: 参数共享促进跨模态语义对齐，MS-CLIP在保持高效性的同时显著提升多模态表征迁移能力，为视觉-语言预训练提供新方向。

In addition, our approach outperforms vanilla CLIP by 1.6 points in linear probing on a collection of 24 downstream vision tasks. Furthermore, we discover that sharing parameters leads to semantic concepts from different modalities being encoded more closely in the embedding space, facilitating the transferring of common semantic structure (e.g., attention patterns) from language to vision. Code is available at \href{https://github.com/Hxyou/MSCLIP}{URL}. 大规模多模态对比预训练已证明通过将多种模态映射到共享嵌入空间来学习一系列下游任务的可转移特征具有很大的实用性。通常，这对每种模态采用单独的编码器。然而，最近的研究表明，变压器可以支持多种模式的学习并允许知识共享。受此启发，我们研究了各种模态共享对比语言图像预训练（MS-CLIP）框架。更具体地说，我们询问在对比预训练期间可以跨模态共享 Transformer 模型的多少参数，并严格检查架构设计选择，以确定沿频谱共享的参数比例。在研究条件下，我们观察到视觉和语言信号的基本统一的编码器优于分离更多参数的所有其他变体。此外，我们发现轻量级特定于模态的并行模块进一步提高了性能。实验结果表明，所提出的 MS-CLIP 方法在零样本 ImageNet 分类（在 YFCC-100M 上预训练）中相对于普通 CLIP 的性能提高了 13%，同时支持参数的减少。此外，在 24 个下游视觉任务的线性探测中，我们的方法比普通 CLIP 的性能高出 1.6 个点。此外，我们发现共享参数导致来自不同模态的语义概念在嵌入空间中被更紧密地编码，从而促进公共语义结构（例如，注意力模式）从语言到视觉的转移。代码可在 \href{https://github.com/Hxyou/MSCLIP}{URL} 获取。 本文提出一种跨模态共享参数的对比语言-图像预训练框架MS-CLIP，通过统一编码器和轻量级模块提升视觉表征学习效果。 Haoxuan You,Luowei Zhou,Bin Xiao,Noel Codella,Yu Cheng,Ruochen Xu,Shih-Fu Chang,Lu Yuan cs.CV,cs.CL

authors_text: Junbo Yin,Jianbing Shen,Xin Gao,David Crandall,Ruigang Yang
title: Graph Neural Network and Spatiotemporal Transformer Attention for 3D Video Object Detection from Point Clouds
title_zh: 用于点云 3D 视频对象检测的图神经网络和时空变换器注意力
comment: ef:IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence, 2021
comments: ef:IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence, 2021
abstract: Previous works for LiDAR-based 3D object detection mainly focus on the single-frame paradigm. In this paper, we propose to detect 3D objects by exploiting temporal information in multiple frames, i.e., the point cloud videos. We empirically categorize the temporal information into short-term and long-term patterns. To encode the short-term data, we present a Grid Message Passing Network (GMPNet), which considers each grid (i.e., the grouped points) as a node and constructs a k-NN graph with the neighbor grids. To update features for a grid, GMPNet iteratively collects information from its neighbors, thus mining the motion cues in grids from nearby frames. To further aggregate the long-term frames, we propose an Attentive Spatiotemporal Transformer GRU (AST-GRU), which contains a Spatial Transformer Attention (STA) module and a Temporal Transformer Attention (TTA) module. STA and TTA enhance the vanilla GRU to focus on small objects and better align the moving objects. Our overall framework supports both online and offline video object detection in point clouds. We implement our algorithm based on prevalent anchor-based and anchor-free detectors. The evaluation results on the challenging nuScenes benchmark show the superior performance of our method, achieving the 1st on the leaderboard without any bells and whistles, by the time the paper is submitted.
summary: Previous works for LiDAR-based 3D object detection mainly focus on the single-frame paradigm. In this paper, we propose to detect 3D objects by exploiting temporal information in multiple frames, i.e., the point cloud videos. We empirically categorize the temporal information into short-term and long-term patterns. To encode the short-term data, we present a Grid Message Passing Network (GMPNet), which considers each grid (i.e., the grouped points) as a node and constructs a k-NN graph with the neighbor grids. To update features for a grid, GMPNet iteratively collects information from its neighbors, thus mining the motion cues in grids from nearby frames. To further aggregate the long-term frames, we propose an Attentive Spatiotemporal Transformer GRU (AST-GRU), which contains a Spatial Transformer Attention (STA) module and a Temporal Transformer Attention (TTA) module. STA and TTA enhance the vanilla GRU to focus on small objects and better align the moving objects. Our overall framework supports both online and offline video object detection in point clouds. We implement our algorithm based on prevalent anchor-based and anchor-free detectors. The evaluation results on the challenging nuScenes benchmark show the superior performance of our method, achieving the 1st on the leaderboard without any bells and whistles, by the time the paper is submitted.
abstract_zh: Previous works for LiDAR-based 3D object detection mainly focus on the single-frame paradigm. In this paper, we propose to detect 3D objects by exploiting temporal information in multiple frames, i.e., the point cloud videos. We empirically categorize the temporal information into short-term and long-term patterns.为了对短期数据进行编码，我们提出了一个网格消息传递网络（GMPNet），它将每个网格（即分组点）视为一个节点，并与相邻网格构建一个 k-NN 图。为了更新网格的特征，GMPNet 迭代地从其邻居收集信息，从而从附近的帧中挖掘网格中的运动线索。为了进一步聚合长期帧，我们提出了一种注意力时空变换器 GRU（AST-GRU），其中包含空间变换器注意（STA）模块和时间变换器注意（TTA）模块。 STA and TTA enhance the vanilla GRU to focus on small objects and better align the moving objects. Our overall framework supports both online and offline video object detection in point clouds. We implement our algorithm based on prevalent anchor-based and anchor-free detectors.在具有挑战性的 nuScenes 基准上的评估结果显示了我们的方法的卓越性能，在论文提交时，在没有任何花哨的情况下在排行榜上排名第一。

AI.tldr: 本文提出结合图神经网络和时空Transformer注意力机制，利用点云视频中的时序信息进行3D目标检测。
AI.motivation: 现有基于LiDAR的3D目标检测方法主要关注单帧数据，未能充分利用多帧点云视频中的时序信息。本文旨在通过挖掘短时和长时时序模式提升检测性能。
AI.method: 提出网格消息传递网络（GMPNet）处理短时信息，通过k-NN图迭代更新网格特征；设计包含空间Transformer注意力（STA）和时序Transformer注意力（TTA）的AST-GRU模块聚合长时信息，增强小目标和运动物体对齐能力。
AI.result: 在nuScenes基准测试中，该方法在无需额外技巧的情况下达到排行榜第一，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过融合时空注意力机制，框架能有效捕捉点云视频中的动态特征，适用于在线和离线场景，为多帧3D目标检测提供新思路。

authors_text: Junbo Yin,Jin Fang,Dingfu Zhou,Liangjun Zhang,Cheng-Zhong Xu,Jianbing Shen,Wenguan Wang
title: Semi-supervised 3D Object Detection with Proficient Teachers
title_zh: 由熟练教师进行半监督 3D 物体检测
abstract: Dominated point cloud-based 3D object detectors in autonomous driving scenarios rely heavily on the huge amount of accurately labeled samples, however, 3D annotation in the point cloud is extremely tedious, expensive and time-consuming. To reduce the dependence on large supervision, semi-supervised learning (SSL) based approaches have been proposed. The Pseudo-Labeling methodology is commonly used for SSL frameworks, however, the low-quality predictions from the teacher model have seriously limited its performance. In this work, we propose a new Pseudo-Labeling framework for semi-supervised 3D object detection, by enhancing the teacher model to a proficient one with several necessary designs. First, to improve the recall of pseudo labels, a Spatialtemporal Ensemble (STE) module is proposed to generate sufficient seed boxes. Second, to improve the precision of recalled boxes, a Clusteringbased Box Voting (CBV) module is designed to get aggregated votes from the clustered seed boxes. This also eliminates the necessity of sophisticated thresholds to select pseudo labels. Furthermore, to reduce the negative influence of wrongly pseudo-labeled samples during the training, a soft supervision signal is proposed by considering Box-wise Contrastive Learning (BCL). The effectiveness of our model is verified on both ONCE and Waymo datasets. For example, on ONCE, our approach significantly improves the baseline by 9.51 mAP. Moreover, with half annotations, our model outperforms the oracle model with full annotations on Waymo.
summary: Dominated point cloud-based 3D object detectors in autonomous driving scenarios rely heavily on the huge amount of accurately labeled samples, however, 3D annotation in the point cloud is extremely tedious, expensive and time-consuming. To reduce the dependence on large supervision, semi-supervised learning (SSL) based approaches have been proposed. The Pseudo-Labeling methodology is commonly used for SSL frameworks, however, the low-quality predictions from the teacher model have seriously limited its performance. In this work, we propose a new Pseudo-Labeling framework for semi-supervised 3D object detection, by enhancing the teacher model to a proficient one with several necessary designs. First, to improve the recall of pseudo labels, a Spatialtemporal Ensemble (STE) module is proposed to generate sufficient seed boxes. Second, to improve the precision of recalled boxes, a Clusteringbased Box Voting (CBV) module is designed to get aggregated votes from the clustered seed boxes. This also eliminates the necessity of sophisticated thresholds to select pseudo labels. Furthermore, to reduce the negative influence of wrongly pseudo-labeled samples during the training, a soft supervision signal is proposed by considering Box-wise Contrastive Learning (BCL). The effectiveness of our model is verified on both ONCE and Waymo datasets. For example, on ONCE, our approach significantly improves the baseline by 9.51 mAP. Moreover, with half annotations, our model outperforms the oracle model with full annotations on Waymo.
abstract_zh: 自动驾驶场景中基于点云的 3D 物体检测器在很大程度上依赖于大量精确标记的样本，然而，点云中的 3D 注释极其繁琐、昂贵且耗时。为了减少对大型监督的依赖，人们提出了基于半监督学习（SSL）的方法。伪标签方法通常用于 SSL 框架，然而，教师模型的低质量预测严重限制了其性能。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种用于半监督 3D 对象检测的新伪标签框架，通过几个必要的设计将教师模型增强为熟练的模型。首先，为了提高伪标签的召回率，提出了时空集成（STE）模块来生成足够的种子框。其次，为了提高召回框的精度，设计了基于聚类的框投票（CBV）模块来从聚类的种子框中获取聚合投票。这也消除了选择伪标签的复杂阈值的必要性。此外，为了减少训练过程中错误伪标记样本的负面影响，通过考虑盒式对比学习（BCL）提出了软监督信号。我们模型的有效性在 ONCE 和 Waymo 数据集上得到了验证。例如，在 ONCE 上，我们的方法将基线显着提高了 9.51 mAP。此外，在半注释的情况下，我们的模型优于 Waymo 上带有完整注释的 oracle 模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种半监督3D目标检测框架，通过改进教师模型减少对大量标注数据的依赖。
AI.motivation: 现有基于点云的3D检测方法依赖大量标注数据，而3D标注成本高昂且伪标签质量低限制了半监督学习效果。
AI.method: 设计空间时序集成模块生成种子框、聚类投票模块提升精度，并引入盒级对比学习减弱错误伪标签影响。
AI.result: 在ONCE数据集上提升基线9.51 mAP，使用半标注数据时在Waymo数据集上超越全标注模型。
AI.conclusion: 该框架通过优化伪标签生成与筛选机制，有效提升了半监督3D检测性能并降低标注需求。

Moreover, with half annotations, our model outperforms the oracle model with full annotations on Waymo. 自动驾驶场景中基于点云的 3D 物体检测器在很大程度上依赖于大量精确标记的样本，然而，点云中的 3D 注释极其繁琐、昂贵且耗时。为了减少对大型监督的依赖，人们提出了基于半监督学习（SSL）的方法。伪标签方法通常用于 SSL 框架，然而，教师模型的低质量预测严重限制了其性能。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种用于半监督 3D 对象检测的新伪标签框架，通过几个必要的设计将教师模型增强为熟练的模型。首先，为了提高伪标签的召回率，提出了时空集成（STE）模块来生成足够的种子框。其次，为了提高召回框的精度，设计了基于聚类的框投票（CBV）模块来从聚类的种子框中获取聚合投票。这也消除了选择伪标签的复杂阈值的必要性。此外，为了减少训练过程中错误伪标记样本的负面影响，通过考虑盒式对比学习（BCL）提出了软监督信号。我们模型的有效性在 ONCE 和 Waymo 数据集上得到了验证。例如，在 ONCE 上，我们的方法将基线显着提高了 9.51 mAP。此外，在半注释的情况下，我们的模型优于 Waymo 上带有完整注释的 oracle 模型。 本文提出一种半监督3D目标检测框架，通过改进教师模型减少对大量标注数据的依赖。 Junbo Yin,Jin Fang,Dingfu Zhou,Liangjun Zhang,Cheng-Zhong Xu,Jianbing Shen,Wenguan Wang cs.CV

authors_text: Junbo Yin,Dingfu Zhou,Liangjun Zhang,Jin Fang,Cheng-Zhong Xu,Jianbing Shen,Wenguan Wang
title: ProposalContrast: Unsupervised Pre-training for LiDAR-based 3D Object Detection
title_zh: ProposalContrast：基于 LiDAR 的 3D 物体检测的无监督预训练
abstract: Existing approaches for unsupervised point cloud pre-training are constrained to either scene-level or point/voxel-level instance discrimination. Scene-level methods tend to lose local details that are crucial for recognizing the road objects, while point/voxel-level methods inherently suffer from limited receptive field that is incapable of perceiving large objects or context environments. Considering region-level representations are more suitable for 3D object detection, we devise a new unsupervised point cloud pre-training framework, called ProposalContrast, that learns robust 3D representations by contrasting region proposals. Specifically, with an exhaustive set of region proposals sampled from each point cloud, geometric point relations within each proposal are modeled for creating expressive proposal representations. To better accommodate 3D detection properties, ProposalContrast optimizes with both inter-cluster and inter-proposal separation, i.e., sharpening the discriminativeness of proposal representations across semantic classes and object instances. The generalizability and transferability of ProposalContrast are verified on various 3D detectors (i.e., PV-RCNN, CenterPoint, PointPillars and PointRCNN) and datasets (i.e., KITTI, Waymo and ONCE).
summary: Existing approaches for unsupervised point cloud pre-training are constrained to either scene-level or point/voxel-level instance discrimination. Scene-level methods tend to lose local details that are crucial for recognizing the road objects, while point/voxel-level methods inherently suffer from limited receptive field that is incapable of perceiving large objects or context environments. Considering region-level representations are more suitable for 3D object detection, we devise a new unsupervised point cloud pre-training framework, called ProposalContrast, that learns robust 3D representations by contrasting region proposals. Specifically, with an exhaustive set of region proposals sampled from each point cloud, geometric point relations within each proposal are modeled for creating expressive proposal representations. To better accommodate 3D detection properties, ProposalContrast optimizes with both inter-cluster and inter-proposal separation, i.e., sharpening the discriminativeness of proposal representations across semantic classes and object instances. The generalizability and transferability of ProposalContrast are verified on various 3D detectors (i.e., PV-RCNN, CenterPoint, PointPillars and PointRCNN) and datasets (i.e., KITTI, Waymo and ONCE).
abstract_zh: 现有的无监督点云预训练方法仅限于场景级或点/体素级实例辨别。场景级方法往往会丢失对于识别道路物体至关重要的局部细节，而点/体素级方法本质上受到有限的感受野的影响，无法感知大型物体或上下文环境。考虑到区域级表示更适合 3D 对象检测，我们设计了一种新的无监督点云预训练框架，称为 ProposalContrast，它通过对比区域提案来学习鲁棒的 3D 表示。具体来说，通过从每个点云采样的一组详尽的区域提案，对每个提案内的几何点关系进行建模，以创建富有表现力的提案表示。为了更好地适应 3D 检测属性，ProposalContrast 通过簇间和提议间分离进行优化，即增强跨语义类和对象实例的提议表示的辨别力。 ProposalContrast 的通用性和可迁移性在各种 3D 检测器（即 PV-RCNN、CenterPoint、PointPillars 和 PointRCNN）和数据集（即 KITTI、Waymo 和 ONCE）上得到验证。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于LiDAR的3D目标检测无监督预训练框架ProposalContrast，通过对比区域提案学习鲁棒的3D表征。
AI.motivation: 现有无监督点云预训练方法在场景级或点/体素级实例区分中存在局限性，无法同时保留局部细节和感知大物体/环境上下文。
AI.method: 通过生成区域提案并建模提案内几何点关系，结合类间与实例间分离优化，实现区域级表征学习。
AI.result: 在PV-RCNN、CenterPoint等多检测器及KITTI、Waymo等数据集上验证了方法的泛化性和迁移性。
AI.conclusion: ProposalContrast通过区域级对比学习有效提升了3D目标检测的表征能力，具有良好的通用性。

authors_text: Bingjie,Xu,Yunan Wu,Pengxiao Hao,Marc Vermeulen,Alicia McGeachy,Kate Smith,Katherine Eremin,Georgina Rayner,Giovanni Verri,Florian Willomitzer,Matthias Alfeld,Jack Tumblin,Aggelos Katsaggelos,Marc Walton
title: Can Deep Learning Assist Automatic Identification of Layered Pigments From XRF Data?
title_zh: 深度学习能否帮助从 XRF 数据自动识别分层颜料？
abstract: X-ray fluorescence spectroscopy (XRF) plays an important role for elemental analysis in a wide range of scientific fields, especially in cultural heritage. XRF imaging, which uses a raster scan to acquire spectra across artworks, provides the opportunity for spatial analysis of pigment distributions based on their elemental composition. However, conventional XRF-based pigment identification relies on time-consuming elemental mapping by expert interpretations of measured spectra. To reduce the reliance on manual work, recent studies have applied machine learning techniques to cluster similar XRF spectra in data analysis and to identify the most likely pigments. Nevertheless, it is still challenging for automatic pigment identification strategies to directly tackle the complex structure of real paintings, e.g. pigment mixtures and layered pigments. In addition, pixel-wise pigment identification based on XRF imaging remains an obstacle due to the high noise level compared with averaged spectra. Therefore, we developed a deep-learning-based end-to-end pigment identification framework to fully automate the pigment identification process. In particular, it offers high sensitivity to the underlying pigments and to the pigments with a low concentration, therefore enabling satisfying results in mapping the pigments based on single-pixel XRF spectrum. As case studies, we applied our framework to lab-prepared mock-up paintings and two 19th-century paintings: Paul Gauguin's Poèmes Barbares (1896) that contains layered pigments with an underlying painting, and Paul Cezanne's The Bathers (1899-1904). The pigment identification results demonstrated that our model achieved comparable results to the analysis by elemental mapping, suggesting the generalizability and stability of our model.
summary: X-ray fluorescence spectroscopy (XRF) plays an important role for elemental analysis in a wide range of scientific fields, especially in cultural heritage. XRF imaging, which uses a raster scan to acquire spectra across artworks, provides the opportunity for spatial analysis of pigment distributions based on their elemental composition. However, conventional XRF-based pigment identification relies on time-consuming elemental mapping by expert interpretations of measured spectra. To reduce the reliance on manual work, recent studies have applied machine learning techniques to cluster similar XRF spectra in data analysis and to identify the most likely pigments. Nevertheless, it is still challenging for automatic pigment identification strategies to directly tackle the complex structure of real paintings, e.g. pigment mixtures and layered pigments. In addition, pixel-wise pigment identification based on XRF imaging remains an obstacle due to the high noise level compared with averaged spectra. Therefore, we developed a deep-learning-based end-to-end pigment identification framework to fully automate the pigment identification process. In particular, it offers high sensitivity to the underlying pigments and to the pigments with a low concentration, therefore enabling satisfying results in mapping the pigments based on single-pixel XRF spectrum. As case studies, we applied our framework to lab-prepared mock-up paintings and two 19th-century paintings: Paul Gauguin's Poèmes Barbares (1896) that contains layered pigments with an underlying painting, and Paul Cezanne's The Bathers (1899-1904). The pigment identification results demonstrated that our model achieved comparable results to the analysis by elemental mapping, suggesting the generalizability and stability of our model.
abstract_zh: X 射线荧光光谱 (XRF) 在元素分析中发挥着重要作用，在广泛的科学领域，特别是在文化遗产领域。 XRF 成像使用光栅扫描来获取艺术品的光谱，为根据元素组成对颜料分布进行空间分析提供了机会。然而，传统的基于 XRF 的颜料识别依赖于专家对测量光谱的解释进行的耗时的元素映射。为了减少对手工工作的依赖，最近的研究应用机器学习技术在数据分析中对相似的 XRF 光谱进行聚类，并识别最可能的颜料。然而，自动颜料识别策略直接处理真实绘画的复杂结构仍然具有挑战性，例如，颜料混合物和层状颜料。此外，由于与平均光谱相比噪声水平较高，基于 XRF 成像的像素级颜料识别仍然是一个障碍。因此，我们开发了基于深度学习的端到端颜料识别框架，以完全自动化颜料识别过程。特别是，它对底层颜料和低浓度颜料具有高灵敏度，因此能够在基于单像素 XRF 光谱的颜料绘图中获得令人满意的结果。作为案例研究，我们将我们的框架应用于实验室准备的模型画和两幅 19 世纪的画作：保罗·高更的 Poèmes Barbares (1896)，其中包含分层颜料和底层绘画，以及保罗·塞尚的《沐浴者》(1899-1904)。颜料鉴定结果表明，我们的模型取得了与元素图分析相当的结果，表明我们的模型具有普遍性和稳定性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的端到端框架，用于自动识别X射线荧光（XRF）数据中的分层颜料，以提高艺术品分析的效率和准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统XRF颜料识别依赖专家手动分析，耗时且难以处理复杂结构（如颜料混合层）和高噪声单像素数据，亟需自动化解决方案。
AI.method: 开发了深度学习框架，通过单像素XRF光谱直接识别低浓度颜料，结合实验室模拟画作与19世纪真迹（如高更、塞尚作品）进行验证。
AI.result: 模型在分层颜料识别上表现与专家元素映射分析相当，证明其泛化性与稳定性，尤其在低浓度颜料检测中效果显著。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习方法有效解决了XRF数据中复杂颜料结构与噪声问题，为艺术品自动化分析提供了可靠工具。

As case studies, we applied our framework to lab-prepared mock-up paintings and two 19th-century paintings: Paul Gauguin's Poèmes Barbares (1896) that contains layered pigments with an underlying painting, and Paul Cezanne's The Bathers (1899-1904). The pigment identification results demonstrated that our model achieved comparable results to the analysis by elemental mapping, suggesting the generalizability and stability of our model. X 射线荧光光谱 (XRF) 在元素分析中发挥着重要作用，在广泛的科学领域，特别是在文化遗产领域。 XRF 成像使用光栅扫描来获取艺术品的光谱，为根据元素组成对颜料分布进行空间分析提供了机会。然而，传统的基于 XRF 的颜料识别依赖于专家对测量光谱的解释进行的耗时的元素映射。为了减少对手工工作的依赖，最近的研究应用机器学习技术在数据分析中对相似的 XRF 光谱进行聚类，并识别最可能的颜料。然而，自动颜料识别策略直接处理真实绘画的复杂结构仍然具有挑战性，例如，颜料混合物和层状颜料。此外，由于与平均光谱相比噪声水平较高，基于 XRF 成像的像素级颜料识别仍然是一个障碍。因此，我们开发了基于深度学习的端到端颜料识别框架，以完全自动化颜料识别过程。特别是，它对底层颜料和低浓度颜料具有高灵敏度，因此能够在基于单像素 XRF 光谱的颜料绘图中获得令人满意的结果。作为案例研究，我们将我们的框架应用于实验室准备的模型画和两幅 19 世纪的画作：保罗·高更的 Poèmes Barbares (1896)，其中包含分层颜料和底层绘画，以及保罗·塞尚的《沐浴者》(1899-1904)。颜料鉴定结果表明，我们的模型取得了与元素图分析相当的结果，表明我们的模型具有普遍性和稳定性。 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的端到端框架，用于自动识别X射线荧光（XRF）数据中的分层颜料，以提高艺术品分析的效率和准确性。 Bingjie,Xu,Yunan Wu,Pengxiao Hao,Marc Vermeulen,Alicia McGeachy,Kate Smith,Katherine Eremin,Georgina Rayner,Giovanni Verri,Florian Willomitzer,Matthias Alfeld,Jack Tumblin,Aggelos Katsaggelos,Marc Walton cs.CV,cs.LG,eess.IV

authors_text: Wenyun Li,Chi-Man Pun
title: Asymmetric Scalable Cross-modal Hashing
title_zh: 非对称可扩展跨模式哈希
abstract: Cross-modal hashing is a successful method to solve large-scale multimedia retrieval issue. A lot of matrix factorization-based hashing methods are proposed. However, the existing methods still struggle with a few problems, such as how to generate the binary codes efficiently rather than directly relax them to continuity. In addition, most of the existing methods choose to use an $n\times n$ similarity matrix for optimization, which makes the memory and computation unaffordable. In this paper we propose a novel Asymmetric Scalable Cross-Modal Hashing (ASCMH) to address these issues. It firstly introduces a collective matrix factorization to learn a common latent space from the kernelized features of different modalities, and then transforms the similarity matrix optimization to a distance-distance difference problem minimization with the help of semantic labels and common latent space. Hence, the computational complexity of the $n\times n$ asymmetric optimization is relieved. In the generation of hash codes we also employ an orthogonal constraint of label information, which is indispensable for search accuracy. So the redundancy of computation can be much reduced. For efficient optimization and scalable to large-scale datasets, we adopt the two-step approach rather than optimizing simultaneously. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets: Wiki, MIRFlickr-25K, and NUS-WIDE, demonstrate that our ASCMH outperforms the state-of-the-art cross-modal hashing methods in terms of accuracy and efficiency.
summary: Cross-modal hashing is a successful method to solve large-scale multimedia retrieval issue. A lot of matrix factorization-based hashing methods are proposed. However, the existing methods still struggle with a few problems, such as how to generate the binary codes efficiently rather than directly relax them to continuity. In addition, most of the existing methods choose to use an $n\times n$ similarity matrix for optimization, which makes the memory and computation unaffordable. In this paper we propose a novel Asymmetric Scalable Cross-Modal Hashing (ASCMH) to address these issues. It firstly introduces a collective matrix factorization to learn a common latent space from the kernelized features of different modalities, and then transforms the similarity matrix optimization to a distance-distance difference problem minimization with the help of semantic labels and common latent space. Hence, the computational complexity of the $n\times n$ asymmetric optimization is relieved. In the generation of hash codes we also employ an orthogonal constraint of label information, which is indispensable for search accuracy. So the redundancy of computation can be much reduced. For efficient optimization and scalable to large-scale datasets, we adopt the two-step approach rather than optimizing simultaneously. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets: Wiki, MIRFlickr-25K, and NUS-WIDE, demonstrate that our ASCMH outperforms the state-of-the-art cross-modal hashing methods in terms of accuracy and efficiency.
abstract_zh: 跨模态哈希是解决大规模多媒体检索问题的成功方法。提出了许多基于矩阵分解的哈希方法。然而，现有的方法仍然面临一些问题，例如如何有效地生成二进制代码而不是直接将其放松到连续性。此外，现有方法大多数选择使用$n\times n$相似度矩阵进行优化，这使得内存和计算负担不起。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的非对称可扩展跨模态哈希（ASCMH）来解决这些问题。它首先引入集体矩阵分解来从不同模态的核化特征中学习公共潜在空间，然后借助语义标签和公共潜在空间将相似性矩阵优化转化为距离-距离差异问题最小化。因此，减轻了$n\times n$非对称优化的计算复杂度。在生成哈希码时，我们还采用了标签信息的正交约束，这对于搜索准确性是必不可少的。因此可以大大减少计算的冗余。为了高效优化并可扩展到大规模数据集，我们采用两步方法而不是同时优化。对三个基准数据集：Wiki、MIRFlickr-25K 和 NUS-WIDE 的广泛实验表明，我们的 ASCMH 在准确性和效率方面优于最先进的跨模态哈希方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新型非对称可扩展跨模态哈希方法（ASCMH），以解决大规模多媒体检索中的二值码生成效率低和计算复杂度高的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有跨模态哈希方法在生成二值码时直接松弛为连续优化，效率低下；且使用n×n相似度矩阵导致内存和计算成本过高，难以处理大规模数据。
AI.method: 通过集合矩阵分解学习不同模态的公共潜在空间，结合语义标签将相似度矩阵优化转化为距离差最小化问题，并引入标签正交约束和两步优化策略降低计算冗余。
AI.result: 在Wiki、MIRFlickr-25K和NUS-WIDE三个基准数据集上，ASCMH在准确率和效率方面均优于现有最先进的跨模态哈希方法。
AI.conclusion: ASCMH通过公共潜在空间建模和优化策略改进，有效解决了跨模态哈希中的计算复杂度和检索精度问题，具有良好的可扩展性。

authors_text: Yoshiki Ito,Quan Kong,Kenichi Morita,Tomoaki Yoshinaga
title: Efficient and Accurate Skeleton-Based Two-Person Interaction Recognition Using Inter- and Intra-body Graphs
title_zh: 使用体内和体内图进行高效、准确的基于骨骼的两人交互识别
abstract: Skeleton-based two-person interaction recognition has been gaining increasing attention as advancements are made in pose estimation and graph convolutional networks. Although the accuracy has been gradually improving, the increasing computational complexity makes it more impractical for a real-world environment. There is still room for accuracy improvement as the conventional methods do not fully represent the relationship between inter-body joints. In this paper, we propose a lightweight model for accurately recognizing two-person interactions. In addition to the architecture, which incorporates middle fusion, we introduce a factorized convolution technique to reduce the weight parameters of the model. We also introduce a network stream that accounts for relative distance changes between inter-body joints to improve accuracy. Experiments using two large-scale datasets, NTU RGB+D 60 and 120, show that our method simultaneously achieved the highest accuracy and relatively low computational complexity compared with the conventional methods.
summary: Skeleton-based two-person interaction recognition has been gaining increasing attention as advancements are made in pose estimation and graph convolutional networks. Although the accuracy has been gradually improving, the increasing computational complexity makes it more impractical for a real-world environment. There is still room for accuracy improvement as the conventional methods do not fully represent the relationship between inter-body joints. In this paper, we propose a lightweight model for accurately recognizing two-person interactions. In addition to the architecture, which incorporates middle fusion, we introduce a factorized convolution technique to reduce the weight parameters of the model. We also introduce a network stream that accounts for relative distance changes between inter-body joints to improve accuracy. Experiments using two large-scale datasets, NTU RGB+D 60 and 120, show that our method simultaneously achieved the highest accuracy and relatively low computational complexity compared with the conventional methods.
abstract_zh: 随着姿势估计和图卷积网络的进步，基于骨架的两人交互识别越来越受到关注。尽管准确性逐渐提高，但计算复杂性的增加使其对于现实环境更加不切实际。由于传统方法不能完全代表身体间关节之间的关系，因此精度仍有提高的空间。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于准确识别两人交互的轻量级模型。除了采用中间融合的架构之外，我们还引入了分解卷积技术来减少模型的权重参数。我们还引入了一个网络流，可以考虑身体间关节之间的相对距离变化，以提高准确性。使用两个大型数据集 NTU RGB+D 60 和 120 的实验表明，与传统方法相比，我们的方法同时实现了最高的精度和相对较低的计算复杂度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种轻量级模型，用于准确识别两人间的骨骼交互，通过结合中间融合架构和因子化卷积技术降低计算复杂度。
AI.motivation: 尽管现有方法在精度上有所提升，但计算复杂度高且未能充分表征人体间关节关系，限制了其在现实场景中的应用。
AI.method: 设计包含中间融合架构的轻量模型，引入因子化卷积减少参数量，并通过网络流捕捉人体间关节的相对距离变化以提升精度。
AI.result: 在NTU RGB+D 60和120数据集上，该方法在保持最高精度的同时显著降低了计算复杂度，优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法有效平衡了精度与效率，为实时两人交互识别提供了可行解决方案。

authors_text: Yang Liu,Guanbin Li,Liang Lin
title: Cross-Modal Causal Relational Reasoning for Event-Level Visual Question Answering
title_zh: 事件级视觉问答的跨模态因果关系推理
comment: 17 pages, 9 figures. This work has been accepted by IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI). The datasets, code and models are available at https://github.com/HCPLab-SYSU/CMCIR
comments: 17 pages, 9 figures. This work has been accepted by IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI). The datasets, code and models are available at https://github.com/HCPLab-SYSU/CMCIR
abstract: Existing visual question answering methods often suffer from cross-modal spurious correlations and oversimplified event-level reasoning processes that fail to capture event temporality, causality, and dynamics spanning over the video. In this work, to address the task of event-level visual question answering, we propose a framework for cross-modal causal relational reasoning. In particular, a set of causal intervention operations is introduced to discover the underlying causal structures across visual and linguistic modalities. Our framework, named Cross-Modal Causal RelatIonal Reasoning (CMCIR), involves three modules: i) Causality-aware Visual-Linguistic Reasoning (CVLR) module for collaboratively disentangling the visual and linguistic spurious correlations via front-door and back-door causal interventions; ii) Spatial-Temporal Transformer (STT) module for capturing the fine-grained interactions between visual and linguistic semantics; iii) Visual-Linguistic Feature Fusion (VLFF) module for learning the global semantic-aware visual-linguistic representations adaptively. Extensive experiments on four event-level datasets demonstrate the superiority of our CMCIR in discovering visual-linguistic causal structures and achieving robust event-level visual question answering. The datasets, code, and models are available at https://github.com/HCPLab-SYSU/CMCIR.
summary: Existing visual question answering methods often suffer from cross-modal spurious correlations and oversimplified event-level reasoning processes that fail to capture event temporality, causality, and dynamics spanning over the video. In this work, to address the task of event-level visual question answering, we propose a framework for cross-modal causal relational reasoning. In particular, a set of causal intervention operations is introduced to discover the underlying causal structures across visual and linguistic modalities. Our framework, named Cross-Modal Causal RelatIonal Reasoning (CMCIR), involves three modules: i) Causality-aware Visual-Linguistic Reasoning (CVLR) module for collaboratively disentangling the visual and linguistic spurious correlations via front-door and back-door causal interventions; ii) Spatial-Temporal Transformer (STT) module for capturing the fine-grained interactions between visual and linguistic semantics; iii) Visual-Linguistic Feature Fusion (VLFF) module for learning the global semantic-aware visual-linguistic representations adaptively. Extensive experiments on four event-level datasets demonstrate the superiority of our CMCIR in discovering visual-linguistic causal structures and achieving robust event-level visual question answering. The datasets, code, and models are available at https://github.com/HCPLab-SYSU/CMCIR.
abstract_zh: 现有的视觉问答方法经常受到跨模式虚假相关性和过于简化的事件级推理过程的影响，无法捕获视频中的事件时间性、因果关系和动态。在这项工作中，为了解决事件级视觉问答的任务，我们提出了一个跨模式因果关系推理的框架。特别是，引入了一组因果干预操作来发现跨视觉和语言模式的潜在因果结构。我们的框架名为跨模态因果关系推理（CMCIR），涉及三个模块：i）因果感知视觉语言推理（CVLR）模块，用于通过前门和后门因果干预来协作解开视觉和语言的虚假相关性； ii) 时空变换器（STT）模块，用于捕获视觉和语言语义之间的细粒度交互； iii）视觉语言特征融合（VLFF）模块，用于自适应地学习全局语义感知视觉语言表示。对四个事件级数据集的广泛实验证明了我们的 CMCIR 在发现视觉语言因果结构和实现稳健的事件级视觉问答方面的优越性。数据集、代码和模型可在 https://github.com/HCPLab-SYSU/CMCIR 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种跨模态因果关系推理框架（CMCIR），用于解决事件级视觉问答任务，通过发现视觉与语言模态间的因果结构提升问答性能。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉问答方法存在跨模态虚假相关性问题，且事件级推理过程过于简化，无法捕捉事件的时间性、因果性和动态性。
AI.method: 提出包含三个模块的CMCIR框架：1) 通过前门/后门干预分离视觉-语言虚假关联的CVLR模块；2) 捕捉细粒度语义交互的时空变换器STT模块；3) 自适应学习全局语义表征的VLFF模块。
AI.result: 在四个事件级数据集上验证了CMCIR在发现因果结构和实现鲁棒事件级问答任务中的优越性，代码和模型已开源。
AI.conclusion: 该框架通过因果推理有效解决了跨模态虚假关联问题，在事件级视觉问答任务中取得了显著性能提升。

authors_text: Ashima Garg,Depanshu Sani,Saket Anand
title: Learning Hierarchy Aware Features for Reducing Mistake Severity
title_zh: 学习层次结构感知功能以减少错误严重性
abstract: Label hierarchies are often available apriori as part of biological taxonomy or language datasets WordNet. Several works exploit these to learn hierarchy aware features in order to improve the classifier to make semantically meaningful mistakes while maintaining or reducing the overall error. In this paper, we propose a novel approach for learning Hierarchy Aware Features (HAF) that leverages classifiers at each level of the hierarchy that are constrained to generate predictions consistent with the label hierarchy. The classifiers are trained by minimizing a Jensen-Shannon Divergence with target soft labels obtained from the fine-grained classifiers. Additionally, we employ a simple geometric loss that constrains the feature space geometry to capture the semantic structure of the label space. HAF is a training time approach that improves the mistakes while maintaining top-1 error, thereby, addressing the problem of cross-entropy loss that treats all mistakes as equal. We evaluate HAF on three hierarchical datasets and achieve state-of-the-art results on the iNaturalist-19 and CIFAR-100 datasets. The source code is available at https://github.com/07Agarg/HAF
summary: Label hierarchies are often available apriori as part of biological taxonomy or language datasets WordNet. Several works exploit these to learn hierarchy aware features in order to improve the classifier to make semantically meaningful mistakes while maintaining or reducing the overall error. In this paper, we propose a novel approach for learning Hierarchy Aware Features (HAF) that leverages classifiers at each level of the hierarchy that are constrained to generate predictions consistent with the label hierarchy. The classifiers are trained by minimizing a Jensen-Shannon Divergence with target soft labels obtained from the fine-grained classifiers. Additionally, we employ a simple geometric loss that constrains the feature space geometry to capture the semantic structure of the label space. HAF is a training time approach that improves the mistakes while maintaining top-1 error, thereby, addressing the problem of cross-entropy loss that treats all mistakes as equal. We evaluate HAF on three hierarchical datasets and achieve state-of-the-art results on the iNaturalist-19 and CIFAR-100 datasets. The source code is available at https://github.com/07Agarg/HAF
abstract_zh: 标签层次结构通常可作为生物分类学或语言数据集 WordNet 的一部分先验使用。一些工作利用这些来学习层次结构感知特征，以改进分类器以犯语义上有意义的错误，同时保持或减少总体错误。在本文中，我们提出了一种学习层次感知特征（HAF）的新方法，该方法利用层次结构每个级别的分类器，这些分类器被限制生成与标签层次结构一致的预测。通过使用从细粒度分类器获得的目标软标签最小化詹森-香农散度来训练分类器。此外，我们采用简单的几何损失来约束特征空间几何形状以捕获标签空间的语义结构。 HAF 是一种训练时间方法，可以在保持 top-1 错误的同时改善错误，从而解决交叉熵损失问题，将所有错误视为平等。我们在三个分层数据集上评估 HAF，并在 iNaturalist-19 和 CIFAR-100 数据集上取得了最先进的结果。源代码位于 https://github.com/07Agarg/HAF

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种学习层次感知特征（HAF）的方法，利用标签层次结构减少分类错误的严重性，使模型产生语义上更合理的错误。
AI.motivation: 传统分类器对所有错误一视同仁，而利用标签层次结构可使错误更符合语义关联，从而提升模型实用性。
AI.method: 通过在层次结构各层级约束分类器预测一致性，结合Jensen-Shannon散度最小化与几何损失函数，学习符合标签语义结构的特征表示。
AI.result: 在iNaturalist-19和CIFAR-100等三个层次数据集上取得最先进的结果，且代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: HAF方法在保持top-1误差的同时优化错误分布，有效解决了交叉熵损失对错误平等对待的局限性。

authors_text: Rohan Pratap Singh,Mehdi Benallegue,Mitsuharu Morisawa,Rafael Cisneros,Fumio Kanehiro
title: Learning Bipedal Walking On Planned Footsteps For Humanoid Robots
title_zh: 学习人形机器人按计划脚步双足行走
abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (RL) based controllers for legged robots have demonstrated impressive robustness for walking in different environments for several robot platforms. To enable the application of RL policies for humanoid robots in real-world settings, it is crucial to build a system that can achieve robust walking in any direction, on 2D and 3D terrains, and be controllable by a user-command. In this paper, we tackle this problem by learning a policy to follow a given step sequence. The policy is trained with the help of a set of procedurally generated step sequences (also called footstep plans). We show that simply feeding the upcoming 2 steps to the policy is sufficient to achieve omnidirectional walking, turning in place, standing, and climbing stairs. Our method employs curriculum learning on the complexity of terrains, and circumvents the need for reference motions or pre-trained weights. We demonstrate the application of our proposed method to learn RL policies for 2 new robot platforms - HRP5P and JVRC-1 - in the MuJoCo simulation environment. The code for training and evaluation is available online.
summary: Deep reinforcement learning (RL) based controllers for legged robots have demonstrated impressive robustness for walking in different environments for several robot platforms. To enable the application of RL policies for humanoid robots in real-world settings, it is crucial to build a system that can achieve robust walking in any direction, on 2D and 3D terrains, and be controllable by a user-command. In this paper, we tackle this problem by learning a policy to follow a given step sequence. The policy is trained with the help of a set of procedurally generated step sequences (also called footstep plans). We show that simply feeding the upcoming 2 steps to the policy is sufficient to achieve omnidirectional walking, turning in place, standing, and climbing stairs. Our method employs curriculum learning on the complexity of terrains, and circumvents the need for reference motions or pre-trained weights. We demonstrate the application of our proposed method to learn RL policies for 2 new robot platforms - HRP5P and JVRC-1 - in the MuJoCo simulation environment. The code for training and evaluation is available online.
abstract_zh: 基于深度强化学习 (RL) 的腿式机器人控制器在多个机器人平台的不同环境中行走时表现出了令人印象深刻的鲁棒性。为了在现实环境中应用仿人机器人的强化学习策略，构建一个能够在 2D 和 3D 地形上实现任意方向稳健行走并可由用户命令控制的系统至关重要。在本文中，我们通过学习遵循给定步骤序列的策略来解决这个问题。该策略是在一组程序生成的步骤序列（也称为足迹计划）的帮助下进行训练的。我们证明，只需将接下来的 2 个步骤输入到策略中就足以实现全向行走、原地转身、站立和爬楼梯。我们的方法采用针对复杂地形的课程学习，并避免了对参考运动或预训练权重的需要。我们演示了我们提出的方法在 MuJoCo 仿真环境中学习 2 个新机器人平台（HRP5P 和 JVRC-1）的 RL 策略的应用。培训和评估代码可在线获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度强化学习的人形机器人双足行走控制方法，通过学习遵循预规划的足步序列实现复杂地形下的稳健行走。
AI.motivation: 现有RL方法在真实场景中难以实现任意方向行走、地形适应性和用户指令控制，且依赖预训练模型或参考动作。
AI.method: 采用课程学习生成渐进复杂度的足步计划，仅输入未来两步信息训练策略，无需预设动作或权重。
AI.result: 成功在HRP5P和JVRC-1机器人平台实现全向行走、原地转向、站立和爬楼梯，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过足步序列引导学习，有效提升了人形机器人在复杂地形中的控制能力与泛化性能。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度强化学习的人形机器人双足行走控制方法，通过学习遵循预规划的足步序列实现复杂地形下的稳健行走。
ai_content.motivation: 现有RL方法在真实场景中难以实现任意方向行走、地形适应性和用户指令控制，且依赖预训练模型或参考动作。

search_text: Learning Bipedal Walking On Planned Footsteps For Humanoid Robots 学习人形机器人按计划脚步双足行走 Deep reinforcement learning (RL) based controllers for legged robots have demonstrated impressive robustness for walking in different environments for several robot platforms. To enable the application of RL policies for humanoid robots in real-world settings, it is crucial to build a system that can achieve robust walking in any direction, on 2D and 3D terrains, and be controllable by a user-command. In this paper, we tackle this problem by learning a policy to follow a given step sequence. The policy is trained with the help of a set of procedurally generated step sequences (also called footstep plans). We show that simply feeding the upcoming 2 steps to the policy is sufficient to achieve omnidirectional walking, turning in place, standing, and climbing stairs. Our method employs curriculum learning on the complexity of terrains, and circumvents the need for reference motions or pre-trained weights. We demonstrate the application of our proposed method to learn RL policies for 2 new robot platforms - HRP5P and JVRC-1 - in the MuJoCo simulation environment. The code for training and evaluation is available online. 基于深度强化学习 (RL) 的腿式机器人控制器在多个机器人平台的不同环境中行走时表现出了令人印象深刻的鲁棒性。为了在现实环境中应用仿人机器人的强化学习策略，构建一个能够在 2D 和 3D 地形上实现任意方向稳健行走并可由用户命令控制的系统至关重要。在本文中，我们通过学习遵循给定步骤序列的策略来解决这个问题。该策略是在一组程序生成的步骤序列（也称为足迹计划）的帮助下进行训练的。我们证明，只需将接下来的 2 个步骤输入到策略中就足以实现全向行走、原地转身、站立和爬楼梯。我们的方法采用针对复杂地形的课程学习，并避免了对参考运动或预训练权重的需要。我们演示了我们提出的方法在 MuJoCo 仿真环境中学习 2 个新机器人平台（HRP5P 和 JVRC-1）的 RL 策略的应用。培训和评估代码可在线获取。 本文提出一种基于深度强化学习的人形机器人双足行走控制方法，通过学习遵循预规划的足步序列实现复杂地形下的稳健行走。 Rohan Pratap Singh,Mehdi Benallegue,Mitsuharu Morisawa,Rafael Cisneros,Fumio Kanehiro cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.LG
